Turn your phone into a Google Slides remote

Copresent turns your phone into a Google Slides remote - swipe to advance, notes on screen. Need to co-present? Share one link and up to 10 people drive the same deck from any device. No app or account for guests.

Hey Product Hunt! 👋 I'm the maker of Copresent. So Copresent does two things: 📱 Solo - your phone becomes the remote. Open a link, swipe to advance, notes right there on screen. No dongle. 🤝 Together - share one link and up to 10 people can control the same live deck from any device, with one-tap handoff mid-talk. No app, no account for anyone but the host. The reason it exists: Google locked native co-presenting behind paid Workspace plans. If you're on a free account, there's no built-in way to hand off slide control without the "ok, can you share your screen now?" fumble. Would genuinely love your feedback - especially what would make you actually use it in your next talk or class. I'm here all day to answer anything. 🙏 [...] Subscribe

results.title: Copresent - Remote Control for Google Slides
results.content: Control Google Slides together — no more "next slide please." One person installs the extension; everyone else presents from a single shared link, on any device. Copresent is the simplest way to present Google Slides as a team. Think of it as a shared remote for Google Slides — everyone holds the clicker. Multiple presenters can advance slides, see the live current slide, and read their speaker notes from an iPhone, Android, iPad, or laptop. Only the main presenter installs anything; co-presenters join through a secure link that works instantly in any browser. No downloads, no accounts, no extra apps. KEY FEATURES 🖼️ See the current slide live in your remote — no need to watch the projector 📱 Turn your phone into a remote for Google Slides — swipe to advance, no clicker needed 📝 Read your [...] 💼 Investor pitches — co-founders take turns with zero handoff fumbles 🏫 Town halls & all-hands — multiple speakers, one screen share 🎓 Lectures & workshops — roam the room, control slides from your phone 🎥 Remote meetings — present over Zoom, Google Meet & Microsoft Teams 🎤 Solo presenting — just want a phone clicker and your notes? That works too ABOUT COPRESENT Copresent is the easiest way to co-present and remote-control Google Slides with a team — on stage, in the boardroom, or fully remote. One install, a shared link, and you're in sync. Works with Google Slides. Set up in under 30 seconds. [...] Slides — swipe to advance, no clicker needed 📝 Read your speaker notes on every device 🤼 Up to 10 presenters control one deck, taking turns 🔗 One shared link — co-presenters join instantly, no sign-up 🎥 Works over Zoom, Google Meet & Microsoft Teams 🆓 Free, and set up in under 30 seconds 🖼️ Live Slide Preview 🖼️ - Co-presenters see the current slide, rendered live, right in their remote - Always know what's on screen — from the back of the room or a second monitor - Stays in sync even when your presenting window is in the background - Turn it on by connecting your Google account (optional) 📱 Your Phone, a Google Slides Remote 📱 - Swipe left and right to move through your Google Slides - Remote-control your slides from the stage or the back of the room — no physical clicker needed - Works

results.title: Copresent — Shared Google Slides Control for Co-Presenters
results.content:
SPEAKER NOTES

NEXT →

# Turn your phone into a Google Slides remote.

And when you need to co-present, share the link. Your teammates control the same deck from any device — no install, no account, no app.

Add to Chrome — It's free

Free forever Google Slides Setup under 30s

## see it in action

Watch how quickly your phone becomes the clicker — and how easily your team joins the same deck.

Copresent demo video

## everything you need for flawless delivery

### Swipe-to-advance mobile clicker

The remote is a mobile-friendly web page. Open it on any phone. Swipe left and right. That's it.

### Speaker notes, everywhere

Whoever is driving sees the current slide's speaker notes in real time. Hand off mid-sentence without losing the thread.

### Up to 10 co-presenters [...] 03

### Present from anywhere

Open the link on your phone to use it as a clicker. Or share it with co-presenters — they control the same deck from their computer or phone.

#### As your personal clicker

Your phone is now the remote. Swipe to advance. Glance at speaker notes. Walk the stage or the classroom without being tethered to a laptop. No dongle, no Bluetooth pairing.

#### As a shared remote

Send the link to your team. Up to 10 people can take the clicker during the session — with speaker notes on every device. No more awkward handoffs or "next slide" cues.

## what people actually use it for

However you present — on your own with a phone, or a whole team on one link.

One long deck, front to back 

### Sales & partner teams [...] Do my co-presenters need to install anything?

No. Only the main presenter installs the Chrome extension. Everyone else joins through a link that works in any browser.

Does it work on mobile?

Yes. The remote is a mobile-friendly web page — use it on any phone or tablet.

Does it work with PowerPoint or Keynote?

Not yet — Copresent currently works with Google Slides only.

Is it really free?

Yes. Free forever. No account needed for co-presenters.

Is it secure?

Each session has its own unique link. Your slides stay in Google Slides — Copresent never uploads or stores your deck.

## Your phone is your new clicker.

Install Copresent in 30 seconds and never touch a presenter dongle again.

 Add to Chrome — It's free
